page: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top-10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads and large-block uncached writes both ranking in the top 10%, while large-block uncached reads place in the top 25%. Cryptographic and compression operations, such as AES encryption and Bzip3 decompression, also perform in the top tier. For database workloads, Redis SET operations reach 139.15 million operations per second. Large language model inference speeds are strong to top-tier for small and medium models, though performance drops to average for prompt processing on large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E96as_v7 offers a dense memory profile combined with high-frequency dedicated compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage attachment for persistent data. This server is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise resource planning applications, and memory-bound data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 139.15 million operations per second. It delivers strong performance for small-to-medium LLM inference, though prompt processing for 70B models is average. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large databases, in-memory caching, and data processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96as_v7 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 768 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance for databases and in-memory caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD vCPUs and 768 GB RAM for demanding database workloads.

768.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmarks

Optimized for large-scale databases and in-memory caching
